title: What to know about white tongue before using medicinal herbs
intro: White tongue can result from various factors such as dehydration, smoking, or using certain medications. Certain herbal teas can promote oral health and help maintain a healthy tongue. For example, lemon balm tea has antiviral and antibacterial properties that can reduce inflammation and fight harmful bacteria in the mouth. Additionally, sage tea is known for its astringent qualities, helping to remove excess debris and promote tissue tightening in the mouth. Peppermint tea, with its natural cooling and numbing agents, can soothe inflamed tissue and provide relief from discomfort. Regularly consuming these herbal teas can support a cleaner mouth and potentially reduce the appearance of a white tongue.
definition: White tongue refers to a condition where the surface of the tongue turns white due to the accumulation of dead skin cells, bacteria, or other substances. This condition is usually harmless and can result from factors such as poor oral hygiene, dehydration, or smoking. However, a persistent white tongue can sometimes signal a more serious issue, such as a fungal infection or an underlying medical condition. Therefore, it is important to maintain good oral hygiene and seek medical attention if you notice any unusual changes in your oral health. By doing so, you can ensure your overall health and well-being.
